intent: Docstring-only update in src/oida_code/operator_soak/aggregate.py to align rule-5 wording with the QA/A35 sec.5.8-F tightening (rules 3 and 4 short-circuit before rule 5 fires, so reaching rule 5 implicitly requires false_positive_count < 2 AND false_negative_count < 2). No functional change. No test change. No schema change.
notes: Phase 5.8 (QA/A38): branch + controlled docstring change committed; real audit packet generated into bundle/. Workflow operator-soak.yml dispatched twice: (1) run 24994865500 failed at 27s due to relative-path bug in operator-soak.yml (../oida-target did not exist from $GITHUB_WORKSPACE cwd); (2) run 24995045522 succeeded at 1m56s after path fix in commit 0b7a657 (absolute github.workspace paths). Run 24995045522 outcome: gateway-status=diagnostic_only; gateway-official-field-leak-count=0 (ADR-22 hard wall holds); accepted_claims=0; rejected_claims=1 (C.docstring.no_behavior_delta rejected because pytest tool ran 193ms then errored, emitting no [E.tool.pytest.0] evidence; verifier honestly says 'requested tool ran but emitted no citable evidence; cannot promote pass-2 claims'). Independent forbidden-token scan over the 5 downloaded artefacts returned zero hits across FORBIDDEN_VERDICT_TOKENS. cgpro session phase58-soak labelled this case insufficient_fixture (rationale: replay/tool-evidence path failed to ground the preapproved claim — not a code concern, a fixture/runner issue) with UX scores 1/1/1/2 (notes: artefact only partially actionable due to missing pytest stderr/findings). Claude validated the cgpro response against OperatorLabelEntry + OperatorUxScore Pydantic schemas before writing label.json + ux_score.json on disk; labelled_by/scored_by both cite cgpro session phase58-soak (uuid 69ef3a8c-0198-8394-8f09-14a7b120d192).
